name: run_python
description: Executes a Python code snippet in a sandboxed environment with configurable timeout, optional package installation, and stdin input injection. Returns stdout, stderr, and execution metadata.

code.description: The Python source code to execute. Multi-line code is supported.
code.$comment: Executed in an isolated sandbox; no filesystem persistence between calls unless a mounted volume is configured.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum execution time in seconds before the process is forcefully terminated.
timeout_seconds.$comment: Maximum of 300s (5 minutes) to prevent runaway processes in shared execution environments.

inputs.description: Optional key-value pairs injected as environment variables or stdin data available to the executing script.
inputs.$comment: Values are always strings; numeric or boolean types must be parsed inside the script. Prevents injection of complex objects that could bypass sandboxing.

packages.description: Optional list of PyPI package names to install before executing the code.
packages.$comment: Packages are installed fresh per execution; caching behavior depends on backend configuration. Security-sensitive packages may be blocklisted.

python_version.description: The Python interpreter version to use for execution.
python_version.$comment: Enum restricted to actively maintained CPython versions. Older versions (3.8 and below) excluded due to EOL security concerns.
